problem_addressed: Building generally capable embodied agents that can continuously explore, plan, and develop new skills in open-ended worlds without human intervention.
core_claim: VOYAGER is the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent in Minecraft that can continuously explore the world, acquire diverse skills, and make novel discoveries without human intervention.
key_contributions: An automatic curriculum that maximizes exploration
An ever-growing skill library of executable code for storing and retrieving complex behaviors
A new iterative prompting mechanism that incorporates environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification for program improvement
novelty: VOYAGER is novel as the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent in Minecraft that uses blackbox queries to GPT-4 without fine-tuning, employs an automatic curriculum driven by curiosity, and features an ever-growing skill library with iterative refinement based on multiple feedback types.
method_summary: VOYAGER consists of three key components: an automatic curriculum for open-ended exploration, a skill library for increasingly complex behaviors, and an iterative prompting mechanism that uses code as action space. The agent interacts with GPT-4 via blackbox queries and uses Mineflayer as the low-level controller.
method_components: Skill library for storing and retrieving complex behaviors
Iterative prompting mechanism that incorporates environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification
key_innovation: The iterative prompting mechanism that incorporates environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification for program improvement, allowing the agent to refine its skills over time without human intervention.
assumptions: GPT-4 provides sufficient code generation capabilities (5.7× more unique items than GPT-3.5)
The Minecraft environment can be adequately represented through the provided control primitive APIs
The agent can make meaningful progress through self-driven exploration
theoretical_grounding: The paper provides empirical evidence of VOYAGER's performance compared to baselines, showing it obtains 3.3× more unique items, travels 2.3× longer distances, and unlocks key tech tree milestones up to 15.3× faster than prior SOTA.
stated_limitations: The paper doesn't explicitly state limitations in the abstract.
The system relies on blackbox queries to GPT-4, which might be computationally expensive.
unstated_weaknesses: No discussion of the efficiency of the attention mechanisms used in GPT-4, which is central to the project topic.
No comparison with other approaches in terms of computational efficiency.
The paper doesn't address how the attention mechanisms in the LLM affect the agent's performance efficiency.
No ablation studies to isolate the contribution of each component.
threats_to_validity: The specific Minecraft environment might not generalize to other embodied agent tasks.
The results might be specific to the particular implementation of GPT-4.
No ablation studies to isolate the contribution of each component.
reproducibility: low (no code provided, blackbox dependency on GPT-4)
open_questions: How can the attention mechanisms in the LLM be made more efficient for embodied agent tasks?
What is the computational cost of the iterative prompting mechanism?
How does the skill library scale with the number of skills?
Can the approach be adapted to more efficient LLMs?
future_work: Applying the approach to other environments.
Exploring more efficient ways to interact with LLMs.
gaps_this_enables: Research on using efficient attention mechanisms in embodied agents.
Exploring the trade-offs between model size and computational efficiency.
Developing more efficient ways to interact with LLMs in real-time applications.
relevance_to_topic: The paper is relevant to efficient attention mechanisms as it uses LLMs (which rely on attention mechanisms) for embodied agent tasks, but it doesn't directly address efficiency improvements.
reusable_assets: The skill library concept could be adapted for other embodied agent tasks.
limitations_stated: The paper doesn't explicitly state limitations in the abstract.
The system relies on blackbox queries to GPT-4, which might be computationally expensive.
title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
